2026-04-01T05:10:52.3939499Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T05:10:52.3941369Z Agent: 1. **AzureClouds enum naming convention** - The enum uses UPPER_SNAKE_CASE values like `AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD`, `AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD`, `AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT`. Based on past reviews, this is a known issue pattern (see PR #37881 @azure/arm-search and PR #37890 for the same UPPER_SNAKE_CASE issue).
2026-04-01T05:10:52.3943636Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T05:10:52.3945254Z Agent: 2. **`cloudSetting` in client options** - There's a `cloudSetting?: AzureSupportedClouds` in the `AlertsManagementClientOptionalParams`. In many management SDKs, this follows a pattern of using `AzureClouds` enum with specific naming.
2026-04-01T05:10:52.3946898Z Agent:    
2026-04-01T05:10:52.3947656Z Agent: I should check the guidelines file to see what the expected standards are for these naming conventions.

2026-04-01T05:10:52.3956440Z Agent: 1. **`AzureClouds` enum with UPPER_SNAKE_CASE values** - The enum has values like `AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD`, `AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD`, `AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT`. 2026-04-01T05:10:52.3958642Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T05:10:52.3959385Z Agent: The guidelines don't have a specific rule about UPPER_SNAKE_CASE vs PascalCase for enum values. 
2026-04-01T05:10:52.3960518Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T05:10:52.3962894Z Agent: But looking at the Azure SDK TypeScript conventions, enum values in JS/TS typically use PascalCase. Looking at other KnownXxx enums in this file, they all use PascalCase (e.g., `KnownAlertState.Acknowledged`, `KnownCreatedByType.Application`). The `AzureClouds` enum is inconsistent with this pattern.
2026-04-01T05:10:52.3965262Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T05:10:52.3966884Z Agent: 2. **`ProxyResource_1` interface** - This is flagged by the guidelines: "Avoid `_N` suffixes, for example both `Resource` and `Resource_1` interfaces." The API file shows this interface exists alongside `ProxyResource`, which violates the naming convention rule.
2026-04-01T05:10:52.3968711Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T05:10:52.3970230Z Agent: 3. **`AzureClouds` enum** - The enum values use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE (`AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD`, `AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD`, `AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT`), but the Azure SDK for JS typically uses PascalCase for enum values, making this a tool issue.
2026-04-01T05:10:52.3971922Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T05:10:52.3974502Z Agent: The `ProxyResource_1` duplication clearly violates the naming guidelines with its `_N` suffix. 2026-04-01T05:10:52.4001285Z Agent: Now I have a clearer picture. Let me identify the issues:
2026-04-01T05:10:52.4001632Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T05:10:52.4004063Z Agent: 1. **`ProxyResource_1` naming** (line 618 in the API review file) - There are two `ProxyResource` interfaces: `ProxyResource` and `ProxyResource_1`. `ProxyResource_1` is used by `AlertModification` and `AlertsSummary`. The metadata.json shows `@azure/arm-alertsmanagement!ProxyResource:interface` maps to `Microsoft.AlertsManagement.ProxyResource`, but there's no entry for `ProxyResource_1`. This suggests that `ProxyResource_1` comes from an ARM common types model that's different from the service-specific `ProxyResource`. This is a naming issue that should be fixed in the spec.
2026-04-01T05:10:52.4006183Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T05:10:52.4006364Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T05:10:52.4008608Z Agent: 2. **`AzureClouds` enum UPPER_SNAKE_CASE** (lines 268-272) - The enum values use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE (`AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD`, `AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD`, `AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT`), which breaks the pattern since every other enum in the SDK uses PascalCase. The guidelines explicitly warn against `_N` suffixes like `ProxyResource_1`, indicating duplicated models should be consolidated rather than renamed with numeric suffixes—this is a tool issue that's appeared in other PRs as well.
2026-04-01T05:10:52.4010728Z Agent: 
2026-04-01T05:10:52.4013062Z Agent: For the UPPER_SNAKE_CASE enum values, while the guidelines don't directly address this naming convention, it's been flagged as problematic in previous reviews, so it's worth noting as an inconsistency even if it's not explicitly covered in the validation rules. The `_` prefix guideline doesn't actually apply here since underscores between words in UPPER_SNAKE_CASE are different from leading underscores. But given that this inconsistency was flagged in previous PRs and the enum values don't match the PascalCase convention used everywhere else in the SDK, I should report it as a tool issue.

IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. This will vary based on the \"method\" provided, so ensure you provide the correct ID:\n- Provide a workflow ID or workflow file name (e.g. ci.yaml) for 'get_workflow' method.\n- Provide a workflow run ID for 'get_workflow_run', 'get_workflow_run_usage', and 'get_workflow_run_logs_url' methods.\n- Provide an artifact ID for 'download_workflow_run_artifact' method.\n- Provide a job ID for 'get_workflow_job' method.\n","type":"string"}},"required":["method","owner","repo","resource_id"],"type":"object"},"name":"actions_get","icons":[{"src":"data:image/png;base64,","mimeType":"image/png","theme":"light"},{"src":"data:image/png;base64,","mimeType":"image/png","theme":"dark"}]},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"List GitHub Actions workflows in a repository"},"description":"Tools for listing GitHub Actions resources.\nUse this tool to list workflows in a repository, or list workflow runs, jobs, and artifacts for a specific workflow or workflow run.\n","inputSchema":{"properties":{"method":{"description":"The action to perform","enum":["list_workflows","list_workflow_runs","list_workflow_jobs","list_workflow_run_artifacts"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (default: 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"per_page":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (default: 30, max: 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"resource_id":{"description":"The unique identifier of the resource. This will vary based on the \"method\" provided, so ensure you provide the correct ID:\n- Do not provide any resource ID for 'list_workflows' method.\n- Provide a workflow ID or workflow file name (e.g. ci.yaml) for 'list_workflow_runs' method, or omit to list all workflow runs in the repository.\n- Provide a workflow run ID for 'list_workflow_jobs' and 'list_workflow_run_artifacts' methods.\n","type":"string"},"workflow_jobs_filter":{"description":"Filters for workflow jobs. **ONLY** used when method is 'list_workflow_jobs'","properties":{"filter":{"description":"Filters jobs by their completed_at timestamp","enum":["latest","all"],"type":"string"}},"type":"object"},"workflow_runs_filter":{"description":"Filters for workflow runs. **ONLY** used when method is 'list_workflow_runs'","properties":{"actor":{"description":"Filter to a specific GitHub user's workflow runs.","type":"string"},"branch":{"description":"Filter workflow runs to a specific Git branch. Use the name of the branch.","type":"string"},"event":{"description":"Filter workflow runs to a specific event type","enum":["branch_protection_rule","check_run","check_suite","create","delete","deployment","deployment_status","discussion","discussion_comment","fork","gollum","issue_comment","issues","label","merge_group","milestone","page_build","public","pull_request","pull_request_review","pull_request_review_comment","pull_request_target","push","registry_package","release","repository_dispatch","schedule","status","watch","workflow_call","workflow_dispatch","workflow_run"],"type":"string"},"status":{"description":"Filter workflow runs to only runs with a specific status","enum":["queued","in_progress","completed","requested","waiting"],"type":"string"}},"type":"object"}},"required":["method","owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"actions_list","icons":[{"src":"data:image/png;base64,","mimeType":"image/png","theme":"light"},{"src":"data:image/png;base64,","mimeType":"image/png","theme":"dark"}]},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Get commit details"},"description":"Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository","inputSchema":{"properties":{"include_diff":{"default":true,"description":"Whether to include file diffs and stats in the response. Default is true.","type":"boolean"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"sha":{"description":"Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name","type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo","sha"],"type":"object"},"name":"get_commit","icons":[{"src":"data:image/png;base64,","mimeType":"image/png","theme":"light"},{"src":"data:image/png;base64,","mimeType":"image/png","theme":"dark"}]},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Get file or directory contents"},"description":"Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository","inputSchema":{"properties":{"owner":{"description":"Repository owner (username or organization)","type":"string"},"path":{"default":"/","description":"Path to file/directory","type":"string"},"ref":{"description":"Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"sha":{"description":"Accepts optional commit SHA. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment.
